sn45.8:1.2: “Bhikkhus, I will teach the noble eightfold path to you; I will analyze it.
sn45.8:1.3: Listen to that, attend closely to it, I will speak.”
sn45.8:10.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, quite secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unskillful *dhammas*, enters and dwells in the first *jhāna*, with thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of seclusion [*viveka*].
sn45.8:10.3: With the stilling of thought [*vitakka*] and examination [*vicāra*], he enters and dwells in the second *jhāna*, with internal clarity, with unification [*ekodibhāvaṁ*] of mind, without thought [*avitakkaṁ*], without examination [*avicāraṁ*], with *pīti* and *sukha* born of *samādhi* [*samādhijaṁ*].
sn45.8:10.4: And with the fading of *pīti*, he dwells in equanimity [*upekkhā*], with *sati* [*sato*] and fully aware [*sampajāno*], and experiences *sukha* with the body. He enters and dwells in the third *jhāna*, of which the noble ones say: ‘Equanimous [*upekkhā*], with *sati* [*satimā*], dwelling in *sukha*.’
sn45.8:10.5: With the abandoning of *sukha* and the abandoning of *dukkha*, and with the earlier disappearance of joy and distress, he enters and dwells in the fourth *jhāna*, which is neither *dukkha* nor *sukha*, with purity of equanimity [*upekkhā*] and *sati* [*upekkhāsatipārisuddhiṁ*]—
sn45.8:2.1: “And what, *bhikkhus*, is the noble eightfold path?
sn45.8:3.2: The knowledge of *dukkha*, the knowledge of the arising of *dukkha*, the knowledge of the cessation of *dukkha*, the knowledge of the way of practice leading to the cessation of *dukkha*—
sn45.8:4.2: The intention of renunciation, the intention of non-ill will, the intention of non-harming—
sn45.8:5.2: Abstaining from false speech, abstaining from divisive speech, abstaining from harsh speech, abstaining from idle chatter—
sn45.8:6.2: Abstaining from taking life, abstaining from taking what is not given, abstaining from sexual activity—
sn45.8:7.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a noble disciple, having abandoned wrong livelihood, makes a living by right livelihood—
sn45.8:8.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* generates desire, makes an effort, arouses energy, raises up the mind [*citta*], and strives for the non-arising of bad, unskillful *dhammas* that have not arisen;
sn45.8:8.3: for the abandoning of bad, unskillful *dhammas* that have arisen, he generates desire …
sn45.8:8.4: for the arising of skillful *dhammas* that have not arisen, he generates desire …
sn45.8:8.5: for the stability, non-confusion, greater growth, expansion, development [*bhāvanā*], and fulfillment of skillful *dhammas* that have arisen, he generates desire, makes an effort, arouses energy, raises up the mind [*citta*], and strives—
sn45.8:9.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* dwells contemplating body in body, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed desire and distress regarding the world;
sn45.8:9.3: dwells contemplating *vedanā* in *vedanā*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed desire and distress regarding the world;
sn45.8:9.4: dwells contemplating mind [*citta*] in mind [*citta*], ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed desire and distress regarding the world;
sn45.8:9.5: dwells contemplating *dhammas* in *dhammas*, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with *sati* [*satimā*], having removed desire and distress regarding the world—
